description: Therapeutic targets for genetic diseases - oncogenes, tumor suppressors, and monogenic disease genes. Knowledge for target selection and validation.

facts.context: TP53 (p53) is mutated in over 50% of cancers. Its normal function is:
facts.truth: tumor suppressor that activates cell cycle arrest and apoptosis in response to DNA damage
distractors: structural protein with no signaling role
growth factor receptor on cell surface

facts.context: BRCA1/BRCA2 mutations increase cancer risk because these genes normally:
facts.truth: mediate homologous recombination repair of double-strand DNA breaks

facts.context: KRAS G12C mutations are oncogenic because the mutant protein:
facts.truth: is locked in GTP-bound active state, constitutively signaling proliferation
distractors: acts as a tumor suppressor when mutated

facts.context: The BCR-ABL fusion in chronic myeloid leukemia creates:
facts.truth: constitutively active tyrosine kinase driving proliferation (target of imatinib)
distractors: cell surface receptor for growth factors

facts.truth: transcriptionally activating genes for proliferation, metabolism, and ribosome biogenesis

facts.context: Duchenne muscular dystrophy is caused by mutations in DMD gene. The protein dystrophin:
facts.truth: links cytoskeleton to extracellular matrix, stabilizing muscle fiber membranes

facts.context: Cystic fibrosis is caused by CFTR mutations. CFTR protein normally:
facts.truth: acts as chloride channel regulating ion and water transport in epithelia
distractors: is a structural protein in cilia

facts.context: Huntington's disease is caused by CAG repeat expansion in HTT gene. The pathogenic mechanism is:
facts.truth: toxic gain-of-function from polyglutamine aggregates in neurons
distractors: loss of normal huntingtin function only

facts.context: Spinal muscular atrophy is caused by SMN1 loss. SMN2 differs by:
facts.truth: C-to-T change causing exon 7 skipping, producing mostly non-functional protein

facts.context: Sickle cell disease is caused by HBB E6V mutation. The therapeutic approach using gene editing:
facts.truth: reactivates fetal hemoglobin (HBF) by disrupting BCL11A erythroid enhancer

facts.context: EGFR-mutant lung cancers respond to tyrosine kinase inhibitors because:
facts.truth: tumors are oncogene-addicted and depend on mutant EGFR signaling for survival
distractors: EGFR mutations make cells more sensitive to chemotherapy
inhibitors directly kill cells regardless of EGFR status
wild-type EGFR is more sensitive to inhibition

facts.truth: inhibits E2F transcription factors, blocking G1 to S phase transition
